title: Achilles's Best Race
tagline: The Inter-Olympus Championship. The biggest race of the year. Achilles's heel has been aching since Tuesday. He said it's fine.
arc_note: ACHILLES HEEL ARC — Second major episode. The ache is real this time, not just a loose strap. He races anyway and wins — but the last two hundred meters cost him something. The running joke stays ('I'm fine') but begins to carry weight. Athena watches his stride in the final stretch.

characters.focus: Achilles — first real test of the heel arc. He wins the race. The win matters. What he notices after matters more.
support: Zeus (entered race, did not win, had wonderful time)
characters.running_joke_evolution: This episode: 'I'm fine. My heel is fine. I won, didn't I?' — new addition to the catchphrase. Defensive now.

scenes.title: Tuesday
scenes.narration: Tuesday the heel ached. Wednesday it didn't. Thursday it did. Friday — race day — it was fine. Achilles told everyone it was fine four times before breakfast. Nobody had asked. He checked his sandal strap. It was perfectly secured — Athena had re-checked it herself after EP006. The strap wasn't the problem. He didn't know what the problem was. He lined up at the starting mark with seventeen other racers and pushed the thought away.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Achilles at the starting line of a large formal race — seventeen other competitors, official banners, crowd. His expression is focused but there's something else — he checks his sandal once more. The strap is perfect. He rolls his ankle slightly, testing. Then the crowd roars. He steps up. He puts the thought away. 16:9.

scenes.title: He Goes First
scenes.narration: For the first eight hundred meters, everything was perfect. Full speed, sparkle trails, the crowd cheering. He was three hundred meters ahead of second place by the halfway mark. Perseus was somehow second — winged sandals behaving for once. Zeus was running joyfully in fifteenth place and waving at spectators. Achilles didn't look back. He never needed to.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Achilles mid-race at full speed, sparkle trails blazing, massive lead. Perseus in second, winged sandals controlled for once, genuinely racing. Zeus in the middle of the pack waving at spectators while running, grinning. The track below Olympus, crowd on both sides. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Last Two Hundred
scenes.narration: At the final two hundred meters, something changed. Not a break — nothing dramatic. A shift. His stride shortened. The sparkle trails thinned slightly. Nobody watching would have noticed. Athena noticed. She was standing at the two-hundred mark with her scroll and she noticed immediately — her chin tapped once, fast. She watched his right leg. She watched his left heel. She didn't call out. He was still winning. She watched.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. SUBTLE MOMENT — Achilles in the final stretch, stride slightly shorter than before, sparkle trails marginally thinner. From the sideline: Athena at the 200m mark, watching with intense focus. One fast chin tap. Her eyes: his left heel. The crowd cheering. She says nothing. He's still winning. She watches. 16:9.

scenes.title: He Wins
scenes.narration: He crossed first. The crowd erupted. He won by sixty meters — enormous. Perseus was second and genuinely pleased with himself. Zeus finished with the biggest smile of anyone in the race despite coming fourteenth. Achilles stood at the finish line, breathed, and rolled his left ankle once. Then he put his arms up. The crowd loved it. He smiled. He meant it. He had won.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Achilles crossing the finish line, arms up, crowd exploding with cheers. Perseus second, genuinely happy. Zeus fourteenth and absolutely thrilled. Achilles standing at the line — arms up, smiling. He rolls his ankle once, very quickly, while the crowd celebrates. Only Athena sees it from the sideline. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena and the Scroll
scenes.narration: After the ceremony, Athena found him at the edge of the track. She didn't say anything about the heel. She said: 'Your stride at one-eighty meters changed.' He said: 'I won.' She said: 'Yes. By sixty meters. What would it have been without the change?' He didn't answer. She wrote something in her scroll. She showed it to him: two numbers. His winning margin. And below it, her estimate of what it could have been. He looked at the numbers. He said: 'I'm fine.' She said: 'I know you are. I'm writing it down anyway.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. QUIET SCENE — Athena and Achilles at the edge of the empty track after the ceremony. She shows him the scroll. Two numbers. He reads them. His expression: something complicated behind 'I'm fine.' She's not arguing. She's recording. Archie on her shoulder. 16:9.

scenes.title: He Says It Differently
scenes.narration: Later, when Zeus and Perseus asked how he felt after the win, Achilles said: 'I'm fine. My heel is fine. I won, didn't I?' The new addition: 'I won, didn't I.' Zeus said: 'You absolutely won! It was incredible!' Perseus said: 'You were amazing.' Achilles nodded. He looked at his sandal. He said it again, quieter, not really to them: 'I won.' Like he was reminding himself of something.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Zeus and Perseus enthusiastically congratulating Achilles. He says the new version of the line — the 'I won, didn't I' addition. They cheer. He smiles. Then quieter: 'I won.' Looking at his sandal. The running joke, but shifted — it has a note of something underneath it now. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: And here's what this story is about: winning while something hurts isn't the same as winning without anything wrong. Achilles won. He truly won. And Athena wrote it down because she cared about the second number, the bigger one, the one he could reach when the thing that's bothering him gets addressed. She's keeping it for him. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — Achilles with his winner's wreath, genuine and happy. Athena's scroll in the background with the two numbers — his actual margin, and the potential margin. She has added a small note. We don't see what it says. Archie looks at the camera once. 16:9.

lesson: Winning while something's wrong is real. But the people who love you keep track of what you could do without that wrong thing. That's not doubt — that's belief.
achilles_arc_note: EP013: First real heel ache in competition. He wins. Athena records the stride change. The running joke gains its defensive edge. Next: EP016 — the ache gets worse. Season finale: the moment of choice.
youtube_title: Achilles Races in the BIG Championship! (Something's Wrong...) 🏃⚡ | Little Olympus Episode 13
next_episode_preview: Episode 14 — Medusa Makes a Choice! Someone in trouble. Someone Medusa didn't expect to care about. She cares anyway.
